weather_report:
Herdsman wakes under a soft grey lid this late morning; the sun is a rumor behind an even light, and shadows barely form. The air feels settled more than cold, with that hushed, matte clarity you get when clouds smooth the edges of things. Expect little sparkle or glare; UV should stay on the low to moderate side under this blanket, still worth a light touch of protection if you plan to linger outdoors.

The overcast is set to hold through midday and into the afternoon, keeping the day gentle, muted, and perfectly serviceable for ordinary business. Dress in easy layers—a breathable long sleeve or light cardigan over a tee—and skip the heavy sunhat; sunglasses may still help with the flat light. It’s a good window for a steady walk along the lake paths, a quiet loop to the shops for those small errands you’ve been postponing, or a bit of unhurried tidying in the garden without breaking a sweat; indoors, a corner table by a window and a warm drink will suit the soft, even daylight.
